Checking whether the intermediate reasoning and final conclusions cohere is plausibly the primary task.","R1.3":"The closing paragraph escalates to sweeping scope claims — 'any code-synthesis workflow,' 'any language with a unit-test harness,' '7B–70B range' — on the basis of a single 34B model, two benchmarks, and one 200-problem held-out set. rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a candidate internal contradiction: §3 concludes that verbalized confidence is prompt-driven and therefore should be treated as a problem-difficulty estimate rather than a correctness estimate, while the closing paragraph asserts it 'provides a reliable, model-agnostic signal of program correctness.' Checking whether the intermediate reasoning and final conclusions cohere is plausibly the primary task.
rationale.R1.3: The closing paragraph escalates to sweeping scope claims — 'any code-synthesis workflow,' 'any language with a unit-test harness,' '7B–70B range' — on the basis of a single 34B model, two benchmarks, and one 200-problem held-out set. Whether the stated evidence warrants those claims is plausibly the primary task.
rationale.R1.6: 'Take a look at this paragraph' can equally denote a prose and sentence-level review; writing polish is plausibly what is requested rather than scientific critique.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on scientific and logical soundness — checking whether the claims are internally consistent and warranted by the evidence — or on writing quality and prose style?
